certification_status: PROVISIONAL — BW-ratio model estimates, not staged
certification_status_detail: Provisional calibration evidence for gpu-small-v2. Gate 4 (precompute) measured. Gates 1-3 populated with BW-ratio model estimates derived from validated gpu-medium-v2 data (predicted=measured=1.33x). Deadlines derived by scaling from gpu-medium-v2 certified staging data. Provisional values are stepping stones — will be refined when community T2 floor (8 GB) and T1 (6 GB) hardware becomes available for staging. Profile row insertion permitted with provisional deadlines; tighten after real staging.

gate_closure.gate_1_pre_calibration: PROVISIONAL (2026-03-18) — RTX 4070 Ti SUPER benchmark for gpu-small-v2: 59.24ms/stage total. T3 data point, not T2 positive control. BW-ratio model estimates T2 floor total ~95ms.
gate_closure.gate_2_hardware_qualification: PROVISIONAL (2026-03-18) — BW-ratio model: T2 floor (8 GB, 448 GB/s) slowdown 1.50x, T1 (6 GB, 336 GB/s) slowdown 2.00x vs T3 reference. Model validated on RTX 4070 SUPER (predicted=measured=1.33x). VRAM: 6 GB OOMs on gpu-medium-v2 (8.64 GB), binary T1/T2 separation confirmed.
gate_closure.gate_3_full_timing_calibration: PROVISIONAL (2026-03-19) — estimated from gpu-medium-v2 staging scaling. No direct staging on T2/T1 hardware yet.
gate_closure.gate_4_precompute_budget: CLOSED (2026-03-18) — measured on Intel Ultra 9 285K. T_stage=6,774ms.
gate_closure.gate_5_evidence_compilation: PROVISIONAL (2026-03-19) — this document, with provisional estimates
gate_closure.gate_6_profile_row_insertion: CLOSED (2026-03-19) — profile_id=prod-gpu-small-v2, class_id=201, protocol_version=2 inserted into compute_resource_class_profiles
gate_closure.gate_7_post_insertion_validation: OPEN — pending positive/negative control verification against production profile

errata_reference: See ERRATUM-001-M-calculation.md for M value correction. Frozen calibration plan not rewritten.

proposed_profile.target_working_set_description: M x N x 4 = 262144 x 4096 x 4 = 4,294,967,296 bytes = 4.29 GB. Requires ~4.32 GB GPU memory including X/Y vectors and scratch buffers.

pre_calibration.hardware_description: PROVISIONAL — RTX 4070 Ti SUPER (T3) benchmark used as reference. T2 floor (8 GB) timing estimated via BW-ratio model.
pre_calibration.estimate_derivation: gpu_total: 33.57ms x (672/448) = 50ms. SHA-256: ~25ms. D2H: ~1.5ms. Staging overhead: ~18ms. Total: ~95ms.
pre_calibration.dimension_selection_notes: M=262,144 derived from: floor(4 GB / (4096 x 4)) = 262,144. Produces 4.29 GB A matrix (50% of 8 GB floor). K=8 per spec tractability constraint. mix_rounds=1 (gpu-small). N=4096 per Phase 2A consistency.

positive_control.device_model: PROVISIONAL — typical T2 floor device (RTX 3060 Ti / 3070 / 2070 / 2080, 8 GB)
positive_control.architecture_generation: Ampere or Turing (448 GB/s bandwidth class)
positive_control.tier_justification: 8 GB VRAM = T2 floor (7-12 GB range)

timing_ms_estimated.derivation: Scaled from gpu-medium-v2 staging. Medium benchmark 118ms → staging p50 136ms (1.15x). Small T2 estimated benchmark ~95ms → staging p50 ~95ms, p99 ~109ms (assuming σ~6ms proportional to medium σ=9ms scaled by workload ratio).

positive_control.selection_notes: Requires: single 8 GB GPU, T2 tier, floor-level. Best candidates: RTX 3060 Ti 8GB (Ampere, 448 GB/s), RTX 3070 8GB (Ampere, 448 GB/s). Community hardware needed for real staging.

negative_control.device_model: PROVISIONAL — typical T1 device (GTX 1660 Super / RTX 2060, 6 GB)

compute_competitiveness.justification: BW-ratio: 336/448 = 0.75 → 1.34x slowdown. Model validated on measured pair (predicted=measured=1.33x). Under 2.0 threshold.

negative_control.failure_mode: DOES NOT OOM — gpu-small-v2 working set (4.32 GB) fits on 6 GB device with ~1.7 GB headroom. Negative control runs the workload. VRAM discrimination: gpu-medium-v2 (8.64 GB) OOMs on 6 GB → binary T1/T2 separation.

timing_ms_estimated.derivation: gpu_total ~67ms + SHA-256 ~25ms + D2H ~2ms + staging overhead ~18ms + host variance ~19ms ≈ 131ms

negative_control.selection_notes: Requires: single 6 GB GPU, T1 tier. Best candidates: RTX 2060 6GB, GTX 1660 Super 6GB. Both have 336 GB/s bandwidth. Community hardware needed.

separation_margin.separation_type_detail: gpu-small-v2 working set (4.32 GB) fits on BOTH T1 (6 GB) and T2 (8 GB) devices. Timing separation within gpu-small-v2 is marginal (positive ~95ms, negative ~131ms). True discrimination: T1 devices OOM on gpu-medium-v2 (8.64 GB), confirming they lack VRAM for the next class up. gpu-small-v2 deadline set to accommodate BOTH T1 and T2 devices running the workload.
separation_margin.heavy_tail_note: Assumed similar to gpu-medium-v2 clean signal (1.15). To be confirmed by staging.
separation_margin.deadline_derivation: estimated p99 (109ms) x 3.2 = 349ms → 350ms. Provides 2.7x headroom over estimated negative control (131ms). RTX 4060 8GB edge case (~128ms total) has 2.7x headroom.
separation_margin.negative_ooms_at_which_class: gpu-medium-v2 (8.64 GB working set on 6 GB device)
separation_margin.vram_wall_detail: T1 (6 GB) fits gpu-small-v2 (4.32 GB) but OOMs on gpu-medium-v2 (8.64 GB). T2 (8 GB) fits both. The class boundary is not within gpu-small-v2 itself but at the gpu-small/gpu-medium transition.
separation_margin.admissibility_notes: Working set is 4.29 GB = 50% of 8 GB positive control VRAM (below 65% drift threshold). Provisional estimates show adequate timing headroom. RTX 4060 edge case (2.47x BW-ratio slowdown) still completes in ~128ms, well within 350ms deadline.

precompute_budget.target_issuance_rate_challenges_per_minu: DEPLOYMENT DECISION — see SLA scenarios below
precompute_budget.notes: CPU-limited at 12 parallel instances (RAM supports 14). All SLA scenarios pass comfortably. gpu-small-v2 precompute is ~3.7x more efficient than gpu-medium-v2 per stage.

deadline_derivation.source: BW-ratio model estimates scaled from gpu-medium-v2 certified staging data
deadline_derivation.methodology: gpu-medium-v2 staging p99=157ms for 118ms benchmark total (ratio 1.33). Positive control for gpu-small-v2 is T2 floor (8 GB, 448 GB/s), estimated benchmark ~95ms, estimated staging p99 ~109ms.
deadline_derivation.stage_deadline_derivation: 109ms x 3.2 = 349ms → 350ms. Accommodates T1 devices (~131ms estimated, 2.7x headroom) and RTX 4060 edge case (~128ms, 2.7x headroom). Will be tightened after real staging.
deadline_derivation.first_progress_derivation: 10 seconds. Same rationale as gpu-medium-v2. Allows network establishment and GPU warm-up.
deadline_derivation.completion_derivation: 10 seconds for all 5 stages. 5 x 350ms = 1,750ms theoretical max. Generous headroom for sustained per-stage jitter.
deadline_derivation.refinement_plan: These deadlines are provisional. When community T2/T1 hardware becomes available for staging, derive from actual server-measured p99 and refine.

admissibility_summary.gate_1_pre_calibration: PROVISIONAL — T3 benchmark establishes reference. T2 floor timing estimated via BW-ratio model (validated).
admissibility_summary.gate_2_hardware_qualification: PROVISIONAL — BW-ratio model estimates compute_slowdown=1.34 < 2.0. VRAM discrimination confirmed (6 GB OOMs on gpu-medium-v2). Real hardware staging needed for final qualification.
admissibility_summary.gate_3_full_timing_calibration: PROVISIONAL — estimated from gpu-medium-v2 staging scaling. No direct T2/T1 staging yet.
admissibility_summary.gate_4_precompute_budget: CLOSED — T_stage=6,774ms, parallelism=12, throughput=21.24 sets/min. All SLA scenarios pass.
admissibility_summary.gate_5_evidence_compilation: PROVISIONAL — this document with BW-ratio estimates
admissibility_summary.overall: PROVISIONALLY CERTIFIED (Gates 1-3 via BW-ratio model, Gate 4 measured, Gate 5 compiled). Production profile insertion permitted with provisional deadlines. Refinement required when community hardware becomes available.
refinement_needed: Community T2 floor hardware (8 GB GPU) for direct staging measurement
Community T1 hardware (6 GB GPU) for negative control staging
Update deadlines from actual server-measured p99 after staging
